title: Best Dog Potty Training Schedules (2026) – By Age & Breed Size
seoTitle: Dog Potty Training Schedule by Age & Breed | GetPawsy
seoDescription: Potty training schedules for puppies and adult dogs by age and breed size. Printable hourly schedules with feeding and sleep integration.
h1Override: Best Dog Potty Training Schedules – By Age & Breed Size
excerpt: Detailed potty training schedules organized by puppy age and breed size. Hourly breakdowns with feeding integration.
quickAnswer: A potty schedule should be based on your puppy's age: 8 weeks = every 1–2 hours, 12 weeks = every 2–3 hours, 16 weeks = every 3–4 hours. Small breeds need more frequent breaks due to smaller bladders.

faq.question: How often should a 10-week-old puppy go out?
faq.answer: Every 1.5–2 hours during the day, plus after every meal, nap, and play session. That's roughly 10–12 trips per day. It's intensive but temporary.

sections.heading: Schedule by Age
sections.content:
**8–10 Weeks:** Every 1–2 hours. This is the most intensive phase. Expect 10–12 outdoor trips per day. Overnight: 1–2 breaks.

**10–12 Weeks:** Every 2–2.5 hours. You'll see early reliability forming. Overnight: 1 break.

**12–16 Weeks:** Every 2.5–3.5 hours. Most puppies can sleep through the night.

**4–6 Months:** Every 3–4 hours. Reliability should be 90%+ by this stage.

**6+ Months:** Every 4–6 hours. Fully reliable with scheduled meals.

For the full method, read our [Complete Potty Training Guide →](/guides/dog-potty-training-complete-guide). Pair your schedule with [potty training tools →](/collections/dog-potty-training).

sections.heading: Small Breed Adjustments
sections.content:
Toy and small breeds (Chihuahuas, Yorkies, Pomeranians) have smaller bladders and faster metabolisms. Add 30–50% more frequency to the schedules above.

A 12-week Yorkie needs breaks every 1.5–2 hours, not every 2.5–3 hours like a Lab puppy. Small breeds also benefit greatly from [indoor potty solutions →](/collections/dog-potty-training) for apartment living, especially during extreme weather.

For apartment-specific strategies, see our [complete potty training guide →](/guides/dog-potty-training-complete-guide).
